compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Done (commit pending). rebarProvider.jsx: structured per-kind fields (scripted uses+with.<contract field>, agent prompt/mode/model/with, loop var/max_iterations/while/until, map over/as/index_var/max_concurrency) writing one slice of rebar:Config then re-serializing whole blob → round-trips via serializer. Raw JSON kept as 'Advanced (raw JSON)' collapsible (known kinds) + full fallback (unknown kinds); shared source of truth. Field-level errors (empty-required/non-numeric) keep prior value, no silent loss. Branch UX deferred (out of scope); properties panel owned HERE not 4df6. Bundle rebuilt. 290 unit + 5 browser e2e green (round-trip + error + raw-fallback).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
**Completion verification — FAIL**

One acceptance criterion is not met by the implementation.

---

**AC1 — Properties panel renders structured fields per step kind (no raw JSON textarea for the common path).**
MET. `rebarProvider.jsx` renders typed, per-field entries for scripted (uses + with.<contract field> via `WithFieldEntry`), agent (prompt/mode/model/with), loop (var/max_iterations/while/until), and map (over/as/index_var/max_concurrency). The raw JSON textarea is kept only as a collapsible "Advanced (raw JSON)" entry for these known kinds — not the primary path. Unknown kinds fall back to the raw editor as the sole editor.

**AC2 — Properties panel delivered HERE; story 4df6 carries NO properties-panel AC.**
MET. Story 4df6's ACs are exclusively about layout (no overlap), edge waypoints, and expanded sub-processes. The 4df6 body explicitly states: "OUT: properties panel (S8), branch/connection UX (deferred S9), IR semantics." Ownership is verifiable in the 4df6 AC list.

**AC3 — A browser e2e exercises structured editing round-trip (edit a field → IR updates → reload preserves).**
MET. `tests/e2e/js/browser_structured.mjs` drives a headless Chromium session: selects the loop SubProcess, fills the `#bio-properties-panel-rebar-loop-max` field, blurs to commit, reads back the `rebar:Config` blob, asserts `max_iterations == 7`, types a non-numeric value and asserts a field error without corrupting the blob, then saves and asserts the final value round-trips into the IR file. Wired to `test_editor_browser.py::test_editor_structured_fields_roundtrip_error_and_raw_fallback`.

**AC4 — 'Common path' = the known step kinds (scripted/agent/branch/loop/map) get structured fields; an UNKNOWN/uncommon config falls back to the raw JSON editor.**
**NOT MET.** The AC explicitly lists `branch` as a known kind that must receive structured fields. The implementation defines `STRUCTURED_KINDS = ["scripted", "agent", "loop", "map"]` (rebarProvider.jsx:62) — `branch` is absent. In `rebarGroup`, the `branch` case is handled separately with a read-only `when` field and a raw `RawConfigEntry`, not structured fields (rebarProvider.jsx:593–601). The code comment confirms: "Branch UX is deferred (a83a)." While the ticket Scope says "Out: branch UX (deferred)", the AC itself explicitly names branch as a kind that must receive structured fields — the implementation does not deliver that.

**AC5 — Each structured field's setValue writes back into the step's `rebar:Config` blob via the serializer and round-trips; a field-level invalid/empty entry surfaces an error, not silent loss.**
MET. Every structured entry uses `mutateConfig` (rebarProvider.jsx:123–127) as the single write path: parse blob → mutate slice → re-serialize → `updateModdleProperties`. Field-level validation is present on all entry types: `WithFieldEntry.validate` returns "Required" for an empty required field and `coerceTyped` returns a typed error for mismatched values (lines 310–315); `ConfigNumberEntry.validate` returns "Must be an integer" for non-numeric input (lines 395–400); `setValue` guards on `err` and returns early without mutating the blob (lines 316–317, 402–403). The browser e2e asserts that a non-numeric entry shows a field error AND the prior value (7) survives in the blob.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Addressed close-gate finding: branch now gets an EDITABLE structured 'when' condition field (writes rebar:Config.when via mutateConfig round-trip), so all known kinds (scripted/agent/branch/loop/map) have structured fields per AC #4. Deeper branch UX (arms/connections) remains deferred S9. Bundle rebuilt; editor tests green.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
**Completion verification — PASS**

All five acceptance criteria are met by the current implementation.

---

**AC1 — Properties panel renders structured fields per step kind (no raw JSON textarea for the common path).**
MET. `rebarProvider.jsx` line 62 defines `STRUCTURED_KINDS = ["scripted", "agent", "loop", "map"]`. Line 616–621: when a known kind is detected, `structuredEntries(element, kind)` provides typed fields and `AdvancedRawEntry` is added as a collapsible fallback — not the primary UI. Unknown kinds (line 624–630) fall back to the raw JSON editor as the only editor. The raw textarea is not the common path for known kinds.

**AC2 — Properties panel delivered HERE; story 4df6 carries NO properties-panel AC.**
MET. Story 4df6 (`roomy-spine-mirth`) explicitly excludes the properties panel: its description states "OUT: properties panel (S8), branch/connection UX (deferred S9), IR semantics." Its four ACs cover exclusively layout (no overlap), edge waypoints, expanded sub-processes, and the roundtrip-demo fixture — zero overlap with the properties panel.

**AC3 — A browser e2e exercises structured editing round-trip (edit a field → IR updates → reload preserves).**
MET. `tests/e2e/js/browser_structured.mjs` drives a real headless Chromium session: selects the loop SubProcess, fills `#bio-properties-panel-rebar-loop-max`, blurs to commit, reads back `rebar:Config`, asserts `max_iterations == 7`, then types a non-numeric value and asserts a field error without blob corruption. The test (`test_editor_browser.py::test_editor_structured_fields_roundtrip_error_and_raw_fallback`, lines 62–93) wires all three assertions (round-trip, error/no-loss, raw fallback) and asserts the final value persists into the IR file.

**AC4 — Known step kinds (scripted/agent/branch/loop/map) get structured fields; UNKNOWN falls back to raw JSON.**
MET. The prior verification found `branch` absent from `STRUCTURED_KINDS` — this was addressed in the follow-up commit. `WhenEntry` (lines 241–273) is an EDITABLE structured field for branch: it reads `cfg.when` and writes it back via `mutateConfig` (lines 258–262), making it a genuine structured field (not read-only). The `rebarGroup` function (lines 604–613) handles `branch` explicitly: it pushes `WhenEntry` then `RawConfigEntry`. All five named kinds (scripted/agent/branch/loop/map) thus get at least one structured field; truly unknown kinds get only `RawConfigEntry`.

**AC5 — Each structured field's setValue writes back into rebar:Config via serializer and round-trips; a field-level invalid/empty entry surfaces an error, not silent loss.**
MET. `mutateConfig` (lines 123–127) is the shared write path for all structured entries: parse blob → mutate slice → re-serialize → `updateModdleProperties`. Field-level validation is present across all entry types: `WithFieldEntry.validate` returns "Required" for empty required fields and delegates to `coerceTyped` for type errors (lines 321–325); `ConfigTextEntry.validate` returns "Required" for empty required fields (line 370); `ConfigNumberEntry.validate` returns "Must be an integer" for non-integer input (lines 406–412); `WhenEntry` uses `mutateConfig` with conditional clear/set. Every `setValue` guards on `err` and returns early without mutating the blob (lines 327–328, 371–372, 413–414) — no silent loss.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Replace the raw config-JSON textarea now that contracts exist to drive structured fields, completing
the typed-palette authoring experience.

## What
- Structured per-field entries (uses/prompt/mode/model/with/loop bounds) driven by each step kind's
  contract, in `src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor.py` + `editor_assets/`.
- Reconcile with existing story roomy-spine-mirth (editor layout/nesting/properties panel): fold its
  properties-panel scope here; keep its layout work where it is.

## Scope
In: structured fields per step kind + roomy-spine-mirth reconciliation. Out: branch UX (deferred).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The properties panel renders structured fields per step kind (no raw JSON textarea for the common path).
- [ ] the properties panel is delivered HERE; story 4df6 carries NO properties-panel AC (ownership verifiable by 4df6's ACs).
- [ ] A browser e2e exercises structured editing round-trip (edit a field -> IR updates -> reload preserves).
- [ ] 'Common path' = the known step kinds (scripted/agent/branch/loop/map) get structured fields; an UNKNOWN/uncommon config falls back to the raw JSON editor (so 'replace the textarea' means replace-for-known-kinds — reconciling the body).
- [ ] Each structured field's setValue writes back into the step's `rebar:Config` blob via the serializer and round-trips (reload preserves the value); a field-level invalid/empty entry surfaces an error, not silent loss.
compiled_state.title: Properties panel: structured per-field entries (folds in roomy-spine-mirth)
